title: Merck — Contacts
html:
Module 4: Merck Contact Map & Relationship Intelligence

Governing Thought

The Merck Insights organization underwent a major restructuring in 2026, splitting into two business units — Merck Oncology and Merck Specialty, Pharma & Infectious Diseases — under a $3B cost-cutting mandate with 6,000 job reductions. The contact landscape reflects this bifurcation: oncology contacts (Keytruda franchise) operate separately from specialty contacts (HIV, vaccines, transplant). ZoomRx's day-to-day work flows through brand-level managers and directors, while Rhonda Harp (Head of Merck Insights) sits above both BUs as the strategic relationship anchor. Unlike J&J where ZoomRx has 10+ years of deep embedded relationships, the Merck relationship is narrower — concentrated around Keytruda, HIV, and vaccines teams — with significant white space to develop contacts in newer areas (EyeBio, PCSK-9, cardiology). Building relationships at the director level across both BUs is the priority for FY26.

Merck Organization Structure (Post-Restructuring)

Rhonda Harp — Head, Merck Insights (all MR)
  |
  |--- Merck Oncology BU
  |      |--- Keytruda Franchise
  |      |      |--- Evan — Keytruda Client Contact (TB weekly calls, readouts)
  |      |      |--- Ted — Keytruda Team (regular call attendee)
  |      |      |--- Allison — Keytruda Team (Marketing, billing, message confirmation)
  |      |      |--- Rachel [Beecher?] — Keytruda Team (messaging feedback, NCCN messaging advocate)
  |      |      |--- Adit Desai — Merck Oncology (NPP commentary, "EFS + OS combination" messaging)
  |      |      |--- Anil Melathe — Merck Oncology (Imfinzi activity scope questions)
  |      |
  |      |--- [Lynparza team — contacts TBD]
  |
  |--- Merck Specialty, Pharma & Infectious Diseases BU
  |      |--- HIV Franchise
  |      |      |--- [DOR/ISL brand team — contacts TBD]
  |      |--- Vaccines (Gardasil, Enflonsia, VAXELIS, Pneumovax)
  |      |      |--- [Enflonsia brand team — contacts TBD]
  |      |      |--- [Gardasil team — contacts TBD]
  |      |--- Transplant (Prevymis)
  |      |      |--- [Prevymis brand team — contacts TBD]
  |      |--- Cardiology / EyeBio / PCSK-9 (Pipeline)
  |             |--- [Contacts TBD — Rhonda Harp bridge needed]
  |
  |--- Procurement / MSA
         |--- [Merck procurement — AI MSA addendum contact TBD]

Note: The restructuring is recent (1H 2026). Org chart details below the director level are incomplete — many contacts are first-name-only from call notes. Populating full names, titles, and emails is a priority for Dave/Melissa.

Tier 1: Day-to-Day Contacts (Known from Call Notes)

These are people who appear in ZoomRx meeting notes and readout calls. Working-style notes are derived from observed behavior in transcripts.

Keytruda Franchise — Oncology BU

Name Title (Inferred) Email Working Style
Evan Keytruda Client Contact [Populate from Dave/Melissa] Attends TB weekly calls and readouts. Regular presence — appears to be the primary day-to-day liaison for Keytruda tracking work.
Ted Keytruda Team [Populate from Dave/Melissa] Regular call attendee. Consistent presence in Keytruda discussions.
Allison Keytruda Team (Marketing) [Populate from Dave/Melissa] Works with Marketing on billing. Confirms messages. Operationally focused — handles logistics and message validation.
Rachel [Beecher?] Keytruda Team [Populate from Dave/Melissa] Gives feedback on messaging. Pushed for Median OS message renewal. Emphasized NCCN message as unique for the sales team — this is her signature talking point. Analytically engaged, cares about message differentiation.
Adit Desai Merck Oncology [Populate from Dave/Melissa] Commented on NPP. Stated "EFS + OS combination is what we are pushing" — revealing of Merck's current messaging priority. Strategic thinker on the oncology side.
Anil Melathe Merck Oncology [Populate from Dave/Melissa] Asked about Imfinzi activity scope — shows competitive awareness. Likely involved in competitive intelligence or market monitoring.

Cross-Brand / Other BU

Name Title (Inferred) Email Working Style
[HIV brand team] [Populate] [Populate] The $125K global HIV RT win (first global win at Merck) suggests active contacts on the HIV side. Dave/Melissa to provide names.
[Enflonsia/RSV team] [Populate] [Populate] Active PET and Digital Tracking running — day-to-day contacts exist but are not captured in available call notes.
[Prevymis team] [Populate] [Populate] Kidney ATU and KT Patient Qual are active — contacts managing these projects need to be documented.

Tier 2: Decision Makers & Influencers

Senior Leadership

Name Title Email What They Care About
Rhonda Harp Head of Merck Insights (all MR) [Populate from Dave/Melissa] Runs ALL of Merck Insights. Introductory meeting held with ZoomRx. Working to expand capabilities pitch. She is the single most important relationship on the account — the bridge to every new franchise (EyeBio, PCSK-9, cardiology). Budget authority sits here.

Directors and Above

Name Title Email What They Care About
[Keytruda Director] [Populate] [Populate] Likely controls Keytruda MR budget. ZoomRx has director-level relationships per AO plan — need names.
[HIV Director] [Populate] [Populate] IDVYNSO launch (May 2026) creates urgency for this relationship — SFE tracking starts 2H 2026.
[Vaccines Director] [Populate] [Populate] Gardasil revisiting PET — this director controls whether that converts.

Action needed: Dave Johnson notes "relationships at Director level throughout Merck" — these need to be documented with names, emails, and working-style notes. Ask Melissa for the full contact list.

Tier 3: ZoomRx Internal Team (Merck-Facing)

Name Role Cluster Merck Brands
Dave Johnson AO (Principal) — Account owner, all brands
Melissa Weinstein AD (Senior Manager) — Day-to-day account management
Chanakya East 7 Vinoth's cluster Merck execution
Vidhya East 7 Vinoth's cluster Merck execution
Madhur East 7 Vinoth's cluster Merck execution
Jane [Populate cluster] — Keytruda projects
Stuti [Populate cluster] — Keytruda projects
Kopal [Populate cluster] — Merck projects
Revanth [Populate cluster] — Merck projects
David [Populate cluster] — Merck projects
Abhishek [Populate cluster] — Merck projects
Alex Colborn Engagement Manager — Prevymis execution

Relationship Intelligence

Working Style Do's and Don'ts

DO:

  • Lead Rhonda Harp conversations with capabilities, not just renewals. She controls the Insights function — showing ZoomRx can do more than tracking studies (AI, consulting, cross-franchise insights) is the key to account expansion.
  • Reference Rachel's NCCN messaging point in Keytruda readouts. She cares about message differentiation for the sales team — framing findings around NCCN vs. competitor messaging alignment wins her advocacy.
  • Respect the BU split. Oncology and Specialty/Infectious Diseases now operate independently. Don't assume contacts from one BU have visibility into the other. Proposals should be BU-specific.
  • Surface the "EFS + OS combination" framing (Adit Desai's priority) in Keytruda analyses. This is what the oncology team is pushing — aligning ZoomRx readouts to this framing shows responsiveness.
  • Track the AI MSA addendum progress with procurement. This blocks all AI-enabled offerings. Every conversation with Merck procurement should include a status check.

DON'T:

  • Don't pitch AI offerings until the MSA addendum is in place. Merck procurement requires an AI addendum before any AI work can be sold — pitching before it's signed wastes relationship capital.
  • Don't assume Rhonda Harp knows ZoomRx deeply yet. The introductory meeting was recent. She needs to be educated on ZoomRx's full capabilities, not just current project work.
  • Don't conflate the two BUs in proposals. Merck Oncology and Merck Specialty are separate budget centers post-restructuring. A cross-BU proposal requires buy-in from both sides.
  • Don't ignore the cost-cutting context. $3B in cuts and 6,000 job reductions mean every MR dollar is scrutinized. Lead with ROI and cost-efficiency, not just capability.

Meeting Cadences

Cadence Contacts
Weekly (TB calls) Evan, Ted, Allison, Rachel (Keytruda)
Monthly (AO reviews) Dave Johnson, Melissa Weinstein
Quarterly (strategic) Rhonda Harp (target cadence — not yet established)
Ad hoc (readouts) Adit Desai, Anil Melathe, brand teams

Relationship Building Priorities for FY26

High Priority — Invest Now

Contact Why
Rhonda Harp The #1 relationship on the account. She controls all Insights. The introductory meeting was step one — step two is a capabilities showcase that positions ZoomRx as the partner for both BUs. Specifically, she is the bridge to EyeBio and PCSK-9 contacts.
HIV brand team (names TBD) IDVYNSO launches May 2026 with SFE tracking starting 2H 2026. The HIV relationship is growing ($125K first global win) and needs deeper contact mapping.
Merck Procurement (AI addendum) The AI MSA addendum blocks all AI-enabled offerings. Whoever owns procurement for MR vendor agreements is a critical relationship for Dave/Melissa to cultivate.

Medium Priority — Maintain and Deepen

Contact Why
Evan (Keytruda) Day-to-day liaison for the largest brand. Keeping this relationship warm ensures renewals and expansion within the Keytruda franchise.
Rachel (Keytruda) Her messaging feedback is analytically rich and directly actionable. She could become a ZoomRx champion if we consistently deliver on her NCCN and differentiation priorities.
Vaccines contacts (Gardasil, Enflonsia) Gardasil is revisiting PET. Enflonsia PET and Digital are active. These are maintenance relationships that protect base revenue.

Watch List — Monitor for Opportunity

Contact Why
EyeBio team Pipeline asset (ophthalmology). New division, new contacts. Launch services (PET + ATU + Launch KPI) are the pitch — but requires Rhonda Harp introduction.
PCSK-9/Cardiology team Pipeline asset. Same dynamic as EyeBio — new contacts needed, Rhonda Harp is the bridge.
Adit Desai Strategic thinker on oncology side. If he moves into a broader role post-restructuring, he could influence cross-franchise MR decisions.

Contact Gaps to Close

The Merck contact map has significant gaps compared to the J&J gold standard. These need to be filled systematically:

Gap Action Owner Timeline
Full names, titles, emails for Keytruda team (Evan, Ted, Allison, Rachel) Ask Dave/Melissa for complete contact details Vinoth Next 1:1 with Melissa
Rhonda Harp's email and direct reports Populate from introductory meeting follow-up Melissa May 2026
HIV brand team contacts Map ahead of IDVYNSO launch (May 2026) Dave/Melissa Before May 1, 2026
Vaccines team contacts (Gardasil, Enflonsia) Populate from active project teams Melissa May 2026
Prevymis team contacts Populate from Kidney ATU project records Alex Colborn May 2026
Merck Procurement — AI MSA contact Identify and document Dave Urgent
EyeBio / PCSK-9 / Cardiology contacts Rhonda Harp introduction needed Dave/Melissa When pipeline timing clarifies
Post-restructuring org chart (both BUs) Request from Rhonda Harp or Merck IR Dave Q2 2026

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
